Protocol_Name: rTMS 10 Hz Left DLPFC for MDD (Standard)
Evidence_Summary: Guideline-supported by APA, CANMAT (2023), NICE. FDA-cleared protocol basis. Multiple meta-analyses confirm efficacy vs sham.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Figure-8 coil at F3 (10-20 system) or MRI-guided DLPFC
Monitoring_Requirements: PHQ-9 weekly; suicide risk assessment; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Headache/scalp discomfort tracking; seizure monitoring
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If <25% PHQ-9 improvement after 15 sessions: clinician review for parameter adjustment or discontinuation
Notes: Standard FDA-cleared protocol. 10 Hz most studied frequency. Motor threshold calibration required each session.

Protocol_Name: iTBS Left DLPFC for MDD
Evidence_Summary: Non-inferior to 10 Hz rTMS per THREE-D trial (Blumberger 2018, Lancet, n=388). CANMAT 2023 supports. Major advantage: ~3 min vs 37.5 min session.
Intensity: 80% active motor threshold (THREE-D protocol) or 120% RMT
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Figure-8 coil at F3 or MRI-guided DLPFC
Monitoring_Requirements: PHQ-9 weekly; suicide risk assessment; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Same as standard rTMS; slightly shorter exposure
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Same escalation rules as PRO-001
Notes: THREE-D trial: response 49% vs 47% (10Hz); remission 32% vs 27%. Shorter sessions = higher throughput.

Protocol_Name: tDCS Left DLPFC for MDD
On_Label_vs_Off_Label: On-label (Flow FL-100 only for MDD; off-label for other tDCS devices)
Evidence_Summary: Literature-supported. 2024 meta-analysis: SMD=-0.355 vs sham. Flow FL-100 PMA-approved Dec 2025 for moderate-severe MDD (not treatment-refractory).
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Anode at F3; Cathode at Fp2 or right DLPFC
Monitoring_Requirements: PHQ-9 weekly; skin inspection at electrode sites; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Skin irritation/burns; phosphenes; headache; tingling
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If no response after 10 sessions: clinician review
Notes: 2 mA significantly better than 1 mA. Flow FL-100 is ONLY FDA-approved tDCS device. Other tDCS devices are off-label.

Protocol_Name: CES for Depression (Adjunctive)
On_Label_vs_Off_Label: Off-label for depression in US (Alpha-Stim cleared for anxiety/insomnia only)
Evidence_Summary: Emerging for primary MDD. Cochrane 2014: insufficient evidence. Cambridge meta-analysis: g=0.654 but mixed quality. Better evidence for anxiety-predominant presentations.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Ear-clip electrodes
Monitoring_Requirements: PHQ-9 weekly; GAD-7 (anxiety); sleep diary
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Ear discomfort; skin irritation; dizziness (rare)
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If no response after 2 weeks: clinician review
Notes: Alpha-Stim NOT FDA-cleared for depression in US. Depression indication available outside US. Better evidence for anxiety comorbidity.

Protocol_Name: taVNS for Depression (Emerging)
Evidence_Summary: Emerging. 2023 meta-analysis: significant HAMD improvement but GRADE 'low to very low'. Small heterogeneous trials.
Target_Region: Auricular branch of vagus nerve (cymba concha)
Intensity: 0.1-5 mA (subsensory to perceptible); pulse width 200-500 µs
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Ear-clip electrode on cymba concha
Monitoring_Requirements: PHQ-9 weekly; cardiac monitoring if indicated; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Ear discomfort; skin irritation; cough reflex; dizziness
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician-guided parameter adjustment only
Notes: No FDA clearance for depression. Evidence graded 'low to very low' by GRADE. Research protocol only.

Protocol_Name: Neurofeedback Alpha Asymmetry for Depression (Draft)
Evidence_Summary: Emerging with methodological limitations. 2025 review: d=0.38-1.50 but RCTs with blinded assessment show attenuated effects. Placebo response concern.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: EEG electrodes at F3, F4, reference
Monitoring_Requirements: PHQ-9 weekly; qEEG progress tracking; engagement monitoring
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Headache; fatigue; transient mood changes
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Protocol adjustment based on qEEG progress every 10 sessions
Notes: Blinding concerns: RCTs with sham show attenuated effects. Present as clinician-guided adjunctive only.

Protocol_Name: Deep TMS H7-Coil for OCD (FDA-Cleared Protocol)
Evidence_Summary: FDA De Novo cleared Aug 2018. Pivotal RCT (Carmi 2019): 38.1% response vs 11.1% sham. Symptom provocation + dTMS.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: BrainsWay H7-coil (deep TMS)
Monitoring_Requirements: Y-BOCS every 2 weeks; PHQ-9 (comorbid depression); adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Headache; transient anxiety increase during provocation; jaw pain
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Continue to 40 sessions if partial response at 30 sessions (post-hoc analysis supports continued improvement)
Notes: H7-coil targets ACC/mPFC, NOT DLPFC. Symptom provocation is integral. First non-invasive device cleared for OCD.

Protocol_Name: rTMS 1 Hz Bilateral SMA for OCD (Off-Label)
Evidence_Summary: Literature-supported. Meta-analyses show SMA 1 Hz effective for OCD. Double-blind RCT: significant Y-BOCS decrease at 6 weeks, sustained at follow-up.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Figure-8 coil at Fz (SMA target)
Monitoring_Requirements: Y-BOCS every 2 weeks; PHQ-9; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Scalp discomfort; occasional twitching during SMA stimulation
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review after 15 sessions for response assessment
Notes: Off-label protocol. SMA/pre-SMA is primary target. Improvements in compulsions more than obsessions in some studies.

Protocol_Name: CES for Anxiety (Alpha-Stim)
Evidence_Summary: Literature-supported. 2022 meta-analysis: g=-0.625 overall; g=-1.218 primary anxiety. Alpha-Stim: ES=-1.05 (p<0.0001).
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Ear-clip electrodes
Monitoring_Requirements: GAD-7 weekly; PHQ-9 (comorbid depression); adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Ear discomfort; dizziness (rare)
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review after 2 weeks; adjust treatment duration based on response
Notes: Alpha-Stim FDA-cleared for anxiety. Strongest CES evidence is for anxiety.

Protocol_Name: CES for Insomnia (Alpha-Stim)
Evidence_Summary: Emerging-Literature-supported. CES significantly improved insomnia in anxiety-predominant patients (ES=-1.02). Half of high-quality studies show improvement.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Ear-clip electrodes
Monitoring_Requirements: ISI weekly; sleep diary; PHQ-9; GAD-7
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Ear discomfort; dizziness (rare)
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review after 2 weeks
Notes: Alpha-Stim cleared for insomnia. Evidence stronger when anxiety is comorbid. Differentiate primary vs secondary insomnia.

Protocol_Name: rTMS for PTSD (Right DLPFC)
Evidence_Summary: Literature-supported. 2025 meta-analysis: SMD=1.29 (p<0.0001) for PTSD symptoms. Dose-response: more sessions = greater improvement.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Figure-8 coil at F4 (right DLPFC)
Monitoring_Requirements: PCL-5 weekly; PHQ-9; safety screening; trauma-related distress monitoring
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Trauma-related anxiety in clinical setting; headache; scalp discomfort
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review after 10 sessions; consider extending if partial response
Notes: No FDA clearance for PTSD. Both HF and LF protocols studied; no clear superiority. Support during sessions recommended for PTSD patients.

Protocol_Name: rTMS M1 for Chronic Pain / Fibromyalgia
Evidence_Summary: Literature-supported. European IFCN 2020: Level B recommendation. HF-rTMS M1 significantly decreased VAS scores in fibromyalgia. Effects may not be permanent.
Target_Region: Primary Motor Cortex (M1) contralateral to pain
Total_Course: 5-10 sessions; maintenance sessions may be needed
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Figure-8 coil over M1 hand area contralateral to pain
Monitoring_Requirements: VAS/NRS pain pre/post session; BPI; PHQ-9; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Headache; scalp discomfort; temporary pain increase
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If response noted: consider maintenance schedule (weekly or biweekly sessions)
Notes: IFCN Level B for fibromyalgia. M1 targeting for sensory component. DLPFC for affective pain component.

Protocol_Name: tDCS M1 for Chronic Pain / Fibromyalgia
Evidence_Summary: Literature-supported. European IFCN 2017: Level B for fibromyalgia. Home-based tDCS meta-analysis: SMD=-0.95 for chronic pain.
Target_Region: Primary Motor Cortex (M1) anode; contralateral supraorbital cathode
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Anode over M1 contralateral to pain; Cathode contralateral supraorbital
Monitoring_Requirements: VAS/NRS pain pre/post session; BPI; skin inspection; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Skin irritation; tingling; headache; burn risk with poor electrode prep
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review after 10 sessions; consider home-based continuation if response noted
Notes: Level B recommendation for fibromyalgia. Home-based protocols studied with good adherence.

Protocol_Name: sTMS for Acute Migraine (eNeura)
Evidence_Summary: FDA-cleared for acute and preventive migraine. Pivotal RCT: 38% pain-free at 2h vs 17% sham.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Toroidal coil placed at back of head by patient
Monitoring_Requirements: Headache diary; attack frequency; MIDAS/HIT-6 periodic
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Lightheadedness (mild); very low seizure risk (single pulse)
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: N/A — as-needed acute treatment
Notes: Self-administered at home. Single-pulse = very low seizure risk. Prescription required.

Protocol_Name: gammaCore nVNS for Cluster Headache / Migraine
On_Label_vs_Off_Label: On-label (gammaCore cleared for CH and migraine)
Evidence_Summary: FDA-cleared for episodic CH (acute + prevention) and migraine (acute + prevention). PRESTO trial: pain freedom superior to sham.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Gel-coated contact surface applied to neck over vagus nerve
Monitoring_Requirements: Headache diary; attack frequency; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Neck twitching; voice changes; cough; bradycardia (rare)
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: N/A — as-needed use with prescribed dose limits
Notes: Cervical nVNS, not auricular. Self-administered. NOT for chronic cluster headache.

Protocol_Name: VNS Therapy for Drug-Resistant Epilepsy
Evidence_Summary: Guideline-supported. FDA PMA-approved 1997. 60% seizure reduction at 3 years (CORE-VNS). >125,000 implanted worldwide.
Intensity: 0.25-3.5 mA (ramped up over weeks); pulse width 250-500 µs
Session_Duration: Continuous (30s ON / 5 min OFF duty cycle)
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Helical bipolar electrode on left vagus nerve; subcutaneous IPG in chest
Monitoring_Requirements: Seizure diary; AED level monitoring; device interrogation; voice/swallowing assessment
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Hoarseness; cough; dyspnea; throat discomfort; surgical site infection
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Gradual current increase (0.25 mA steps) over weeks to therapeutic level; AutoStim for seizure-triggered stimulation
Notes: Surgical implant. Progressive benefit over time. Not a cure but reduces seizure frequency.

Protocol_Name: ANT-DBS for Drug-Resistant Epilepsy
Evidence_Summary: FDA-approved 2018 (SANTE trial). 68% responder rate (≥50% reduction) at 5 years.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: DBS leads in bilateral ANT; subcutaneous IPG in chest
Monitoring_Requirements: Seizure diary; device interrogation; psychiatric monitoring (depression risk); MRI conditional review
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Memory impairment (15%); depressive mood (13%); surgical risks (hemorrhage 1.8%, infection 4.5%)
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Voltage titration over weeks; programming adjustments based on seizure response
Notes: SANTE trial basis. Psychiatric monitoring important (15% memory, 13% depression). SUDEP case reported in MORE registry.

Protocol_Name: DBS STN/GPi for Parkinson's Disease
Evidence_Summary: Guideline-supported (AAN). 30+ years evidence. 40-60% UPDRS motor improvement. Adaptive DBS (BrainSense) FDA approved Feb 2025.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: DBS leads in bilateral STN or GPi; subcutaneous IPG
Monitoring_Requirements: UPDRS Part III; medication diary; quality of life; psychiatric screening; device interrogation
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Dysarthria; dyskinesia; mood changes; impulse control (STN); surgical risks
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Stimulation parameter optimization over months; medication adjustment post-surgery
Notes: Standard of care for advanced PD. STN: more medication reduction; GPi: less dyskinesia/mood risk.

Protocol_Name: DBS VIM for Essential Tremor
Evidence_Summary: Guideline-supported. FDA-approved 1997. 40-80% tremor reduction in long-term follow-up.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: DBS lead in VIM thalamus
Monitoring_Requirements: Tremor rating scale; functional assessment; speech evaluation (bilateral risk); device interrogation
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Paresthesias; dysarthria (bilateral risk); gait instability; battery replacement
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Stimulation parameter optimization; consider cycling to reduce tolerance
Notes: Alternative: MRgFUS thalamotomy (lesional, irreversible but no hardware). Bilateral DBS increases dysarthria risk.

Protocol_Name: DBS GPi for Dystonia
Evidence_Summary: Guideline-supported for drug-refractory primary dystonia. HDE H020007 (2003) → PMA (2025).
Total_Course: Permanent; benefit may take weeks-months to develop
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: DBS leads in bilateral GPi
Monitoring_Requirements: Burke-Fahn-Marsden scale; quality of life; device interrogation
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Surgical risks; dysarthria; gait changes; mood effects (rare with GPi)
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Gradual parameter optimization; benefit develops over weeks-months (unlike PD/ET where response is rapid)
Notes: Response develops slowly over weeks-months (unlike PD). Genetic testing may predict response (DYT1+).

Protocol_Name: Deep TMS H4-Coil for Smoking Cessation
Evidence_Summary: FDA-cleared 2020. First TMS device cleared for any addiction indication. H4-coil targets bilateral insula/PFC.
Total_Course: 6 weeks (with brief smoking cue exposure before each session)
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: BrainsWay H4-coil
Monitoring_Requirements: Smoking status (exhaled CO); craving VAS; Fagerström; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Headache; jaw pain; scalp discomfort
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Combined with behavioral counseling and/or NRT for best outcomes
Notes: H4-coil different from H7 (OCD) and H1 (depression). Smoking cue exposure protocol integral.

Protocol_Name: taVNS for Opioid Withdrawal (Sparrow)
On_Label_vs_Off_Label: On-label (Sparrow Ascent cleared for opioid withdrawal)
Evidence_Summary: FDA-cleared. Transcutaneous auricular neurostimulation for opioid withdrawal symptom relief. Used with standard symptomatic medications.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Sparrow Ascent ear-clip electrodes
Monitoring_Requirements: COWS; SOWS; symptom tracking; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Ear discomfort; skin irritation
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Used in conjunction with standard withdrawal management
Notes: Only FDA-cleared taVNS device. Specifically for opioid withdrawal, NOT psychiatric indications.

Protocol_Name: rTMS for Post-Stroke Motor Recovery (Inhibitory Contralesional)
Evidence_Summary: Literature-supported. 2024 meta-analysis: significant FMA-UE improvement. Best evidence for subacute phase. Both inhibitory contralesional and excitatory ipsilesional approaches effective.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Figure-8 coil over contralesional M1
Monitoring_Requirements: FMA-UE; Barthel Index; modified Rankin; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Seizure risk (post-stroke); headache; scalp discomfort
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review after 10 sessions; consider ipsilesional excitatory if contralesional insufficient
Notes: Inhibitory contralesional best for less-severe hemiplegia in acute/subacute. Excitatory ipsilesional for severe/chronic.

Protocol_Name: rTMS for Post-Stroke Motor Recovery (Excitatory Ipsilesional)
Evidence_Summary: Literature-supported. Excitatory ipsilesional protocol for severe hemiplegia in chronic phase.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Figure-8 coil over ipsilesional M1
Monitoring_Requirements: FMA-UE; grip strength; ARAT; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Seizure risk (post-stroke); headache
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review after 10 sessions
Notes: Better for severe hemiplegia in chronic phase. Combined with task-specific therapy for best results.

Protocol_Name: Neurofeedback Theta/Beta for ADHD (Clinician Draft — Evidence Caution)
Evidence_Summary: CRITICAL: NOT supported by blinded outcomes. Cortese 2024 (JAMA Psychiatry, n=2472): SMD=0.04 (probably blinded). Effects seen ONLY with unblinded parent-rated measures. Standard protocols show limited signal on blinded outcomes.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: EEG electrodes at Fz/Cz, reference
Monitoring_Requirements: ASRS or Conners/SNAP; BRIEF; CPT; qEEG progress; engagement tracking
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Minimal adverse events; primary concern is opportunity cost vs evidence-based treatments
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: qEEG review every 10 sessions; clinician review of blinded outcome measures
Notes: EVIDENCE CAUTION: SMD=0.04 on blinded outcomes (Cortese 2024). Do NOT present as established treatment. Opportunity cost concern. Inform patients of evidence limitations.

Protocol_Name: rTMS 1 Hz Temporal Cortex for Tinnitus
Evidence_Summary: Emerging. Modest short-term benefit (THI, VAS improvement). NOT durable at 6 months. Laterality affects response.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Figure-8 coil over left temporal/TPJ
Monitoring_Requirements: THI; TFI; VAS loudness/distress; audiogram at baseline
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Headache; scalp discomfort; temporary tinnitus loudness increase
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If no response after 10 sessions: consider combined temporal + prefrontal protocol
Notes: Short-term benefit only. Not durable at 6 months. Left-sided and bilateral tinnitus respond better than right-sided.

Protocol_Name: rTMS for Post-TBI Cognitive Enhancement (Emerging)
Evidence_Summary: Emerging. Small studies and case reports suggest improvement in attention and processing speed. No RCT-level evidence.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Figure-8 coil at F3 (DLPFC)
Monitoring_Requirements: MoCA; Trail Making Test; BRIEF-A; PCL-5 if trauma-related; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Seizure risk (post-TBI); headache; check for skull defects/metallic implants from surgical repair
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review after 10 sessions
Notes: No RCT-level evidence specifically for TBI cognitive enhancement. Screen carefully for post-surgical metallic implants.

Protocol_Name: VNS Therapy for Treatment-Resistant Depression
Evidence_Summary: FDA PMA-approved Jul 2005 for TRD (≥4 adequate Rx failures). Adjunctive long-term treatment.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Helical bipolar electrode on left vagus nerve; subcutaneous IPG
Monitoring_Requirements: MADRS or HAM-D monthly; suicide risk assessment; quality of life; device interrogation
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Hoarseness; cough; dyspnea; surgical risks
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Gradual current increase over weeks; response may take 3-12 months — do not discontinue early
Notes: Response takes months — not weeks. Specifically for ≥4 Rx failures. Surgical implant required.

Protocol_Name: DBS ALIC for Severe OCD (HDE)
Evidence_Summary: HDE-approved 2009 for severe treatment-resistant OCD. Failed ≥3 SSRIs required. Reclaim DBS. Growing evidence base.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: DBS leads in bilateral ALIC
Monitoring_Requirements: Y-BOCS monthly; CGI; depression screening; psychiatric monitoring; device interrogation
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Surgical risks; hypomania risk; mood instability; weight changes
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Gradual parameter optimization by experienced DBS team; psychiatric monitoring mandatory
Notes: HDE = humanitarian device. Requires institutional oversight. Failed ≥3 SSRIs. Only for severe, refractory OCD.

Protocol_Name: rTMS for Parkinson's Disease (Adjunctive)
Evidence_Summary: Literature-supported as adjunct. 2015 JAMA Neurology meta-analysis: significant motor improvement. 2026 meta-analysis: improved UPDRS-III, FOG-Q, MoCA, BDI.
Target_Region: M1 or DLPFC (depending on target symptoms)
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Figure-8 coil over M1 (motor symptoms) or DLPFC (depression/cognition)
Monitoring_Requirements: UPDRS Part III; MoCA; PHQ-9; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: DBS presence is contraindication to TMS; seizure risk; headache
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review based on UPDRS response
Notes: Adjunctive only. DBS is contraindication. Multiple targets depending on symptom profile.

Protocol_Name: rTMS Bilateral Sequential for Anxious Depression
Evidence_Summary: Literature-supported. Retrospective cohort: both unilateral and bilateral produce ~44-45% GAD-7 improvement. Sequential bilateral may address both depression and anxiety.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Figure-8 coil at F3 then F4
Monitoring_Requirements: PHQ-9; GAD-7; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Longer session duration; headache; scalp discomfort
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review after 15 sessions
Notes: For comorbid anxiety-depression presentations. Bilateral sequential addresses both circuits.

Protocol_Name: HRV Biofeedback for Stress and Autonomic Regulation
Evidence_Summary: Literature-supported for stress/anxiety reduction. Often used as adjunctive to primary neuromodulation protocol.
Target_Region: Autonomic nervous system via HRV monitoring
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Heart rate sensor (chest strap or pulse oximeter)
Monitoring_Requirements: HRV metrics; GAD-7; stress diary
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: No significant adverse events
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Identify resonance frequency; progressive home practice
Notes: Adjunctive method. Not a primary neuromodulation treatment. Good for autonomic dysregulation component. Source: Lehrer & Gevirtz 2014 meta-analysis; Gevirtz 2013 review. Parameters vary by device.

Protocol_Name: TPS for Parkinson Disease (Emerging evidence adjunct)
Evidence_Summary: Emerging small trials for adjunct motor symptoms with TPS; not guideline-supported. Registry row supports evidence traceability only.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Handheld applicator with neuronavigation
Monitoring_Requirements: UPDRS Part III; MoCA; PHQ-9; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Headache; sensation; investigational US status
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician milestone review per institutional governance
Notes: Synthetic PD+TPS protocol linkage for API tests — not treatment guidance.

Protocol_Name: tACS 10 Hz Left DLPFC for Treatment-Resistant Depression
Evidence_Summary: RCT underway (NCT06812923, N=52, 4-wk course); 2024 review covers tACS in MDD (PMID 39261427); mechanistic review supports alpha/beta modulation (PMID 33211157). Larger RCTs needed.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: 4x1-ring HD montage centred F3 or standard 2-electrode F3-contralateral
Monitoring_Requirements: EEG at baseline recommended; scalp inspection each session; phosphene threshold check
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Skin redness/tingling log; phosphene diary; no persistent AEs in review (PMID 30214966)
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If no response at 10 sessions: clinician review of frequency and electrode placement
Notes: Two frequency arms in NCT06812923 (10 Hz vs 77.5 Hz). PMID 30214966 AE review applies. CON mapping: CON-002 TRD (closest match in taxonomy; seed doc tagged as CON-MDD F33).

Protocol_Name: tACS 40 Hz Gamma DLPFC for Alzheimer's Disease
Evidence_Summary: Phase 2 pilot (NCT06826261, N=30, 40 Hz, 8 wks, home-based); 2025 narrative review supports gamma entrainment for AD (PMID 40142358). No powered RCT completed yet.
Intensity: NCT opaque on current_ma — verify against Starstim-home protocol paper
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Neuroelectrics Starstim-home; multichannel model-optimised individualized montage
Monitoring_Requirements: Remote digital monitoring portal; caregiver-administered; weekly video check
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Scalp warmth/tingling log; pediatric AE monitoring protocol (PMID 38761518)
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If cognitive decline accelerates: pause and clinician review
Patient_Facing_Allowed: Limited (home-based arm requires caregiver + remote monitoring)
Notes: Frequency (40 Hz), session duration (60 min daily, 30 s ramp up/down), 5 d/wk x 8 wk = 40 sessions confirmed from NCT06826261 arm description + brief summary (ClinicalTrials.gov v2 API). NCT opaque on current_ma — check published Starstim-home protocol paper. CON mapping: CON-017 Cognitive Impairment / TBI (closest; seed doc tagged CON-AD G30, no AD-specific CON in taxonomy).

Protocol_Name: tACS 40 Hz Temporal Lobe for Schizophrenia Auditory Hallucinations
Evidence_Summary: Completed RCT NCT05282329 (N=50, gamma, 5 d/wk x 4 wks, 2 mA, 20 min); completed pilot NCT04545294 (N=36, 6 Hz theta, negative symptoms).
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Standard 2-electrode bilateral temporal (T3/T4 or equivalent)
Monitoring_Requirements: PANSS / AHRS weekly; EEG monitoring at baseline
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Scalp tingling; phosphenes; extrapyramidal symptom check given antipsychotic co-use
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If hallucinations worsen or new neurological symptoms: stop and refer
Notes: No SCZ CON exists in taxonomy (Condition_ID left blank — mismatch flagged per merge rules). Seed doc tagged CON-SCZ F20. Best-controlled tACS dataset for schizophrenia in DB.

Protocol_Name: tACS Theta (6 Hz) Frontoparietal for MCI Working Memory
Evidence_Summary: Ongoing RCT NCT04135742 (N=195, 40 Hz/2 mA/20 min arm with CCT); pilot NCT04545294 (N=36, 6 Hz/2 mA/20 min theta arm). Largest tACS trial in MCI to date.
Sessions_per_Week: 10 sessions/wk (twice-daily x 5 consecutive weekdays per NCT04545294); 2/wk in NCT04135742 40 Hz arm
Total_Course: 10 sessions over 1 week (NCT04545294 6 Hz arm); separate NCT04135742 40 Hz arm = 24 sessions over 3 months + cognitive training
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: 4x1 HD ring multi-electrode (10 carbon rubber electrodes 1 cm radius) on Eldith DC stimulator Plus + Equalizer Box (NeuroConn); 100-cycle ramp; 0 deg phase
Monitoring_Requirements: EEG at baseline; cognitive testing at 0/post
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Tingling/phosphene diary
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Adjust if EEG shows no theta entrainment at session 5
Notes: Parameters now confirmed from NCT04545294 arm description via ClinicalTrials.gov v2 API: 6 Hz, 2 mA, 20 min, twice-daily x 5 weekdays = 10 sessions; 4x1 HD ring montage on Eldith DC stimulator Plus + Equalizer Box (NeuroConn). NCT04135742 confirms separate 40 Hz/2 mA/20 min arm (24 sessions over 3 months, 2/wk + cognitive training). Choose sub-protocol by target oscillation. CON mapping: CON-017 (closest; seed doc tagged CON-MCI F06.7).

Protocol_Name: tPBM 823 nm Bilateral DLPFC for MDD (ELATED-2 Protocol)
Evidence_Summary: ELATED-2 pilot RCT (N=21, double-blind sham-controlled, effect size d=0.90-1.5; PMID 30346890). ELATED-3 multicenter RCT (PMID 35950904) established low-dose inefficacy threshold. Phase 2 NCT05573074 recruiting N=120. No powered confirmatory RCT yet.
Intensity: 36.2 mW/cm² (ELATED-2 per PMID 30346890); NCT05573074 uses different device (tPBM-2.0, 291.7 mW/cm²) — verify per device
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Bilateral DLPFC simultaneous; probe dimensions 28.7 x 2 cm² (ELATED-2)
Monitoring_Requirements: HAM-D17 baseline + biweekly; skin temperature check; eye protection for operators
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Skin warmth/erythema at probe site; report burns or visual symptoms; DB AE table has no PBM entries (absence-of-evidence caveat)
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If <25% HAM-D improvement at 8 sessions: clinician review; do not increase irradiance without protocol amendment
Notes: ELATED-2 verbatim parameters: 823 nm, CW, 36.2 mW/cm², bilateral DLPFC, 2x/wk, 8 wks. ELATED-3 published Sept 2022 (J Clin Psych) — low-dose arm showed inefficacy threshold. Class II 510k devices cleared for MSK pain only; NO FDA clearance for transcranial brain indications (product codes IYO/GZE — verify via accessdata.fda.gov).

Protocol_Name: tPBM 808 nm Forehead for Chronic TBI Cognitive Rehabilitation
Evidence_Summary: NIH-funded review (PMID 29131369) covers TBI/stroke; NCT06956404 recruiting N=70 (808 nm, 18 sessions); 2016 LED review (PMID 28001756). No large RCT completed.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Forehead (F3/F4/Fz scalp surface) via flexible optical fiber + custom cap
Monitoring_Requirements: MoCA/neuropsychological testing pre/post; scalp temperature; skin inspection
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Scalp warmth log; headache diary; no serious AEs in published tPBM trials
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If cognitive performance declines from baseline at mid-point: pause and refer
Notes: NCT06956404 trial-arm values; recruiting 2024-2026. Photosensitising drug screen required.

Protocol_Name: tPBM 1064 nm Right Forehead for Anxiety Disorders
Evidence_Summary: NCT07133893 (N=280, not yet recruiting, 1064 nm, 8 min); pilot PMID 31647775 (GAD N=15, significant anxiolytic effect p<0.001); PMID 19995444 forehead NIR pilot N=10. Weakest evidence tier.
Intensity: NCT opaque on irradiance — verify against published protocol paper / Schiffer device spec (comparator literature: ~250 mW/cm² typical for 1064 nm brain PBM)
Sessions_per_Week: NCT opaque on sessions/wk — verify against published protocol paper
Total_Course: NCT opaque on total session count — NCT07133893 design = 3 in-person visits per arm, balance TBD — verify against published protocol paper
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Right forehead (F4 / right prefrontal) with handheld NIR device
Monitoring_Requirements: GAD-7 / HAM-A baseline + post; no EEG required
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Scalp warmth; mild headache; eye protection mandatory if device near orbital region
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If anxiety scores worsen at 2 wks: stop and review
Notes: Wavelength (1064 nm), region (right forehead), and 8-min session duration confirmed from NCT07133893 device intervention description (ClinicalTrials.gov v2 API). Design = 3 in-person visits with online follow-up (single-blind sham-controlled). NCT opaque on irradiance and total session count — check published protocol paper / Schiffer 2009 device spec. Comparator literature: ~250 mW/cm² typical for 1064 nm brain PBM. Evidence grade D — weakest set.

Protocol_Name: tPBM 810 nm + 635 nm Helmet for Parkinson's Disease (Symbyx Neuro Protocol)
Evidence_Summary: Randomised clinical trial NCT06036433 (Symbyx Neuro helmet; 8-12 wks + exercise). Proof-of-concept PMID 34215216 and 5-yr follow-up PMID 39385144. Promising motor and non-motor improvements.
Frequency_Hz: N/A — CW (red 635 nm and NIR 810 nm sequential)
Intensity: 1137 J/session total (37.44 J/diode red + 19.44 J/diode NIR; 20 diodes)
Total_Course: ~72 sessions over 12 weeks (or 48 sessions over 8 weeks in 8-wk arm)
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Symbyx Neuro LED helmet with 20 LED clusters (each 810 nm NIR + 635 nm red)
Monitoring_Requirements: UPDRS Part III; MoCA; PHQ-9; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Scalp warmth; photosensitiser screen; eye protection
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review per institutional governance; partner Abdomen-Neck PDCare 904 nm protocol is separate
Notes: Protocol combined with exercise in NCT06036433. Companion SYMBYX Abdomen-Neck uses 904 nm superpulsed laser — separate protocol. No FDA clearance for transcranial PD indication.

Protocol_Name: tACS 77.5 Hz Bilateral Forehead for Chronic Insomnia
Evidence_Summary: Multisite RCT Zhu et al. 2024 (PMID 38176353, N=120, 77.5 Hz tACS vs sham, 20 daily sessions × 4 wks) — reported significant improvement in PSQI/ISI at end of treatment and 8-wk follow-up. Largest tACS insomnia RCT to date.
Intensity: 15 mA (verify — parameter from prior WebSearch summary; confirm against PMID 38176353 primary source)
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Forehead electrodes (bilateral prefrontal); reference placement per trial protocol
Monitoring_Requirements: ISI weekly; PSQI at 0/4/8 wks; sleep diary; scalp inspection each session; phosphene check
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Scalp tingling; phosphenes; skin redness; no persistent AEs reported
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If no improvement at 10 sessions: clinician review of electrode placement and frequency
Notes: Zhu et al. 2024 multisite RCT. 77.5 Hz and 15 mA reported in prior WebSearch summary (MERGE-tacs-pbm-summary.md) but NOT directly verified against abstract — flag verify before clinical use. No FDA clearance for tACS in insomnia.

Protocol_Name: tACS Personalized Theta for Parkinson's Disease (Crossover RCT)
Evidence_Summary: Crossover RCT Del Felice et al. 2019 (PMID 30921609) — personalized theta-tACS frequency plus physical therapy vs sham+PT. Frequency individualized based on patient EEG. Small sample, emerging evidence.
Frequency_Hz: Individualized theta (verify: abstract reports personalized theta frequency; specific Hz per patient)
Intensity: verify — intensity not in abstract summary
Session_Duration: verify — session duration not in abstract summary
Sessions_per_Week: verify — sessions/week not in abstract summary
Total_Course: verify — total course / crossover design details per full paper
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Per individual EEG-guided protocol; combined with physical therapy
Monitoring_Requirements: UPDRS Part III; motor assessments; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Scalp tingling; phosphenes; no persistent AEs reported
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review per institutional governance; crossover arm may require washout
Notes: Del Felice et al. personalized theta-tACS + PT crossover RCT. Specific intensity/duration/sessions NOT available from abstract summary — verify against full paper before clinical use. No FDA clearance.

Protocol_Name: Vivistim Paired VNS for Upper-Limb Stroke Rehabilitation
Evidence_Summary: FDA PMA P200051 approved 2021. Pivotal RCT (VNS-REHAB trial PMID 34115889): paired VNS + rehabilitation significantly improved FMA-UE vs sham+rehab (responder rate 47% vs 24%; p=0.003). 266 papers / 93 trials in DB.
Target_Region: Left cervical vagus nerve (implanted cuff electrode)
Session_Duration: Continuous 0.5 s bursts triggered by therapist during movement
Total_Course: Helical bipolar cuff electrode on left cervical vagus; subcutaneous IPG
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: FMA-UE at baseline/post-course; Barthel Index; voice assessment; cardiac monitoring at implant
Monitoring_Requirements: Yes
Contraindication_Check_Required: Hoarseness; cough; dyspnea; neck pain; surgical site infection; cardiac monitoring required (bradycardia risk 0.4%)
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Gradual current ramp-up starting 0.5 mA; session count extensible to 36 per label compassionate-use extension
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Yes (on-label with trained rehabilitation team)
Patient_Facing_Allowed: Yes — neurosurgery implant + occupational therapy pairing required
Source_URL_Secondary: VNS-REHAB RCT basis (Dawson et al. 2021 Lancet Neurology). Pairing with active upper-limb rehabilitation task is required — not stand-alone. Only approved for upper-limb motor deficit. Note: gammaCore (PRO-015) is NON-implanted cervical VNS for migraine/CH — different device class. Condition slug post-stroke-motor used; no CON-015 aphasia mismatch.
Notes: Unreviewed

Protocol_Name: SMR Neurofeedback for Drug-Resistant Epilepsy
Evidence_Summary: Grade B meta-analysis basis. Tan et al. 2009 (Epilepsy Behav) and Sterman 2000 SMR foundational evidence; Monderer et al. 2002 meta-analysis. 243 DB papers. Systematic review: seizure frequency reduced ~30% in responders. SCP training alternative.
Target_Region: Sensorimotor cortex (C3/C4/Cz) — SMR uptraining; or vertex/frontal for SCP
Frequency_Hz: SMR band (12-15 Hz) uptraining; SCP training as alternative protocol
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: EEG electrodes at C3, Cz, C4; reference at mastoid
Monitoring_Requirements: Seizure diary; EEG progress tracking; AED level monitoring; engagement tracking; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: No direct AEs of neurofeedback; primary risk is seizure during session — ensure safety protocol
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Protocol review every 20 sessions based on seizure diary and qEEG progress; escalate to physician if seizure frequency increases
Patient_Facing_Allowed: No — requires trained neurofeedback practitioner and clinical supervision
Notes: SMR uptraining at C3/Cz/C4 is the most replicated NF protocol for epilepsy (Sterman foundational work). SCP (slow cortical potential) training is alternative with comparable evidence. Grade B = moderate RCT/meta-analysis support; not FDA-cleared. AED continuation mandatory. Source note: PMID 29034226 is HRV-related — top DB citation confounded; primary epilepsy NF meta-analyses per Tan 2009 and Monderer 2002.

Protocol_Name: taVNS Adjunct for MDD (Bilateral Auricular Protocol)
Evidence_Summary: Grade B. PMID 29593576 (Fang et al. 2016 RCT, HAMD-17 significant improvement). 2023 meta-analysis (PMID 37230264) confirms HAMD effect vs sham (SMD=-0.74). 333 DB papers. CE-marked devices in EU.
Target_Region: Bilateral auricular cymba concha (auricular branch of vagus nerve)
Intensity: 0.1–2 mA (subsensory to mildly perceptible); pulse width 200–500 µs
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Bilateral ear-clip or adhesive electrode on cymba concha
Monitoring_Requirements: PHQ-9 or HAMD-17 weekly; cardiac monitoring if indicated; skin inspection; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Ear discomfort; skin irritation; occasional cough reflex; rare dizziness; vasovagal monitoring
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician-guided parameter adjustment only; if no PHQ-9 response at 2 wks increase session duration to 60 min
Notes: GRADE evidence quality rated 'low to very low' for MDD per 2023 meta-analysis. No FDA clearance for depression indication. Bilateral use distinguishes from unilateral protocols. Compared to PRO-005 (CSV taVNS for depression EV-C): evidence upgraded to EV-B here per 2023 meta-analysis update. CON-001 MDD; if TRD use PRO-028 VNS implanted. Sources: PMID 29593576 Fang 2016 RCT; PMID 37230264 2023 meta-analysis.

Protocol_Name: tDCS M1-DLPFC for MS-Related Fatigue (IFCN Level B)
Evidence_Summary: IFCN Level B (Lefaucheur et al. 2017 Clin Neurophysiol PMID 27866120; updated 2024). ~20 RCTs for central fatigue in MS. Meta-analysis (Chalah et al. 2020): significant fatigue reduction vs sham. Condition slug: CON-011 (epilepsy slug — mismatch; MS-Fatigue maps closest to CON-011 in evidence DB but JS conditionId ms-fatigue has no CON equivalent; note mismatch).
Target_Region: Primary Motor Cortex (M1) anode + right supraorbital cathode; or bilateral DLPFC for cognitive fatigue
Laterality: Contralateral M1 for motor fatigue; bilateral DLPFC for cognitive fatigue
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Anode over M1 (contralateral to dominant fatigue side) or left DLPFC; cathode right supraorbital / right DLPFC
Monitoring_Requirements: MFIS (Modified Fatigue Impact Scale) or FSS weekly; skin inspection; PHQ-9 (comorbid depression in MS); adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Skin irritation; tingling; headache; electrode site burn risk — good electrode preparation mandatory
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If MFIS improvement <20% after 10 sessions: review placement and add cognitive training simultaneous with stimulation
Notes: IFCN 2017 Level B basis. MS patients may have skull/scalp changes from disease — assess carefully. Two phenotypes: motor fatigue (M1 target) and cognitive fatigue (DLPFC target). Condition ID mismatch flagged: evidence.db slug not available for ms-fatigue; closest DB CON is CON-011. JS conditionId ms-fatigue exists (p-msf-001 Grade C). This CSV entry upgrades documented grade to B per IFCN citation.

Protocol_Name: rTMS Left DLPFC for Schizophrenia Negative Symptoms
Evidence_Summary: IFCN Level B (Lefaucheur et al. 2017/2024 PMID 27866120). ~30 RCTs for negative symptoms. Meta-analysis: significant improvement in PANSS negative subscale (SMD=-0.33 to -0.67). Auditory hallucinations also respond to 1Hz temporal cortex.
Target_Region: Left DLPFC (negative symptoms target) or Left temporal cortex (auditory hallucinations target)
Frequency_Hz: 10 Hz excitatory (negative symptoms) or 1 Hz inhibitory temporal (auditory hallucinations)
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Figure-8 coil at F3 (DLPFC) or left T3-P3 (temporal-parietal for hallucinations)
Monitoring_Requirements: PANSS negative/positive subscales weekly; PHQ-9; adverse event log; antipsychotic medication log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Headache; scalp discomfort; rare seizure risk (lower threshold if clozapine); rare psychosis exacerbation
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review after 15 sessions; if positive symptoms worsen — discontinue and review antipsychotic management
Notes: IFCN Level B for auditory hallucinations (temporal cortex) and negative symptoms (DLPFC). Positive symptoms must be stable before treatment. Clozapine lowers seizure threshold — reduce intensity to 80% RMT. Condition slug CON-023 does not exist in conditions.csv — closest slug schizophrenia-negative used per JS taxonomy. CON mismatch flagged.

Protocol_Name: rTMS M1 / DLPFC for SCI-Related Central Pain
Evidence_Summary: IFCN Level B (Lefaucheur et al. 2017 PMID 27866120). Meta-analysis Nardone et al. 2017 (Spinal Cord): HF-rTMS M1 significantly reduces neuropathic pain VAS in SCI (SMD=-0.61). ~15 RCTs.
Target_Region: Contralateral M1 (leg representation area at Cz/Pz) or left DLPFC for emotional pain component
Total_Course: 10–20 sessions; maintenance schedule considered after response
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Figure-8 coil over contralateral M1 leg area (Cz-Pz region) or F3 DLPFC
Monitoring_Requirements: VAS/NRS pain pre/post session; SCIM (Spinal Cord Independence Measure); PHQ-9 for depression comorbidity; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Headache; scalp discomfort; transient pain change; spasm exacerbation possible — monitor
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If <30% VAS improvement after 10 sessions: switch target to DLPFC or add DLPFC sequential
Notes: IFCN Level B for central pain including SCI. Leg area of M1 is somatotopically appropriate for lower-limb SCI pain. Condition slug CON-008 (chronic pain) used as closest; spinal-cord-injury-pain JS slug has no CSV CON equivalent.

Protocol_Name: tDCS Anodal Left Broca for Post-Stroke Aphasia
Evidence_Summary: IFCN Level B (Lefaucheur et al. 2017 PMID 27866120). ~12 RCTs. Walker et al. 2021 meta-analysis (Neuropsychol Rehabil): anodal tDCS left hemisphere + speech therapy superior to sham+therapy (g=0.39, p=0.003).
Target_Region: Left hemisphere Broca's area (F3/inferior frontal gyrus) anode; right homolog or supraorbital cathode
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Anode over left inferior frontal gyrus (F3 / Broca area); cathode right homolog (F4) or right supraorbital
Monitoring_Requirements: WAB (Western Aphasia Battery) or BNT at baseline/post; session-by-session language task performance; skin inspection
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Skin irritation; tingling; headache; electrode placement critical — speech pathologist co-delivery
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If no naming improvement after 10 sessions: reassess lesion overlap with stimulation site; consider HD-tDCS
Notes: IFCN Level B. tDCS must be delivered concurrent with speech-language therapy for effect (no stand-alone benefit). Ipsilesional anodal is most-studied; contralesional cathodal also applied. JS conditionId post-stroke-aphasia exists but has only rTMS protocol (p-psa-001) — this CSV entry covers tDCS/aphasia gap.

Protocol_Name: HRV Biofeedback for Generalized Anxiety Disorder
Evidence_Summary: Grade B. ~20 RCTs. Lehrer et al. 2020 meta-analysis (Appl Psychophysiol Biofeedback): significant GAD-7 and HRV-LF improvement. Goessl et al. 2017 meta-analysis (Psychol Med): g=0.81 anxiety reduction. Resonance frequency breathing enhances vagal tone.
Target_Region: Autonomic nervous system (cardiac) via resonance frequency breathing + HRV biofeedback
Frequency_Hz: Resonance frequency (~0.1 Hz breathing / 6 breaths per minute)
Sessions_per_Week: 2–3 clinic sessions + daily home practice (5–10 min)
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Heart rate sensor (chest strap or fingertip PPG); biofeedback display; breathing pacer
Monitoring_Requirements: GAD-7 biweekly; HRV frequency domain metrics (LF/HF) at baseline and post-course; sleep diary
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: No significant AEs; caution in severe cardiac arrhythmia
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Identify individual resonance frequency (5–7 breaths/min range); progress home practice by session 3; reassess GAD-7 at session 6
Notes: Goessl 2017 meta-analysis (Psychol Med, g=0.81) specifically for anxiety disorders. Resonance frequency identification is essential first step. Distinguished from PRO-032 (which targets stress/depression primarily). Note: HRV biofeedback is adjunctive — not suitable as sole treatment for severe GAD. Pair with CBT for best outcomes.

Protocol_Name: rTMS for Bipolar Depression (Low-Frequency Right-Sided Protocol)
Evidence_Summary: IFCN Level B mention (Lefaucheur 2017). Multiple small RCTs: Dell'Osso et al. 2009 (Bipolar Disord) n=11; Praharaj et al. 2011 meta-analysis; ~15 RCTs. Right-sided inhibitory preferred over left excitatory to minimize mania switch risk.
Total_Course: 15–20 sessions; monitor for mood elevation throughout
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Figure-8 coil at F4 (right DLPFC)
Monitoring_Requirements: MADRS or HAMD weekly; Young Mania Rating Scale (YMRS) weekly (switch monitoring); PHQ-9; adverse event log; mood stabilizer level monitoring
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Headache; scalp discomfort; rare: hypomania/mania switch — monitor YMRS weekly; agitation
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Stop immediately if YMRS >12 or clear manic symptoms; always co-administer mood stabilizer
Clinician_Review_Required: Yes — psychiatrist mandatory for mood switch monitoring
Notes: Right-sided inhibitory protocol preferred for BD-depression to minimise mania switch (Dell'Osso 2009 PMID 19811529; Praharaj 2011). Left HF-rTMS has higher switch risk. Current manic or mixed episode is absolute contraindication. Condition slug CON-006 (insomnia) mismatch flagged — BD-Depression has no CON in conditions.csv; bipolar-depression JS slug exists. Use JS conditionId bipolar-depression.

Protocol_Name: PBM Whole-Body + LLLT Tender-Point for Fibromyalgia
Evidence_Summary: Grade B. Yeh 2019 meta-analysis (9 RCTs, N=325) SMD=1.18 for pain, SMD=1.16 FIQ. Son 2025 umbrella review: moderate certainty for fatigue (eSMD=1.25). Ribeiro 2023 (N=90, PBMT+sMF, 9 sessions) positive. Navarro-Ledesma 2022 whole-body protocol reproducible.
Frequency_Hz: Continuous-wave 660 nm + 850 nm (Navarro-Ledesma 2022 whole-body); 808–904 nm local
Intensity: 25.2 J/cm² per site (Navarro-Ledesma 2022); LLLT tender-point 6 J (de Carvalho 2012)
Session_Duration: 20 min whole-body (Navarro-Ledesma 2022); ~50 min LLLT + exercise (de Carvalho 2012)
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Whole-body LED bed and/or local laser cluster over tender points per ACR criteria
Monitoring_Requirements: FIQ and pain VAS weekly; tender-point count; fatigue scale
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Transient erythema; eye-protection required; rare photosensitivity
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If <20% FIQ reduction after 6 sessions: reassess dosing and tender-point selection
Notes: Yeh 2019 meta-analysis (PMID 31747571) is anchor — 9 RCTs N=325 large effects. Verify dose/schedule per specific device used. Pair with graded exercise. All patients require eye protection during illumination.

Protocol_Name: tcVNS Cervical Vagus for PTSD (Bremner Protocol)
Evidence_Summary: Grade C. Bremner 2021 RCT (N=20, tcVNS 3 mo twice daily) 31% greater PCL reduction vs sham (p=0.013). Gurel 2020 (N=25) sympathetic attenuation. Benzouak 2025 systematic review: very low certainty overall. Distinct from auricular taVNS (p-ptsd-002) — cervical tcVNS montage.
Target_Region: Cervical (transcutaneous) vagus — gammaCore-style neck placement; OR left cymba conchae (auricular variant)
Frequency_Hz: 30 Hz pulse, 250 µs pulse width (Zhang 2025 protocol); 25 Hz common alternative
Intensity: Start 0.4 V, titrate by 0.4 V to tingle threshold (Zhang 2025)
Sessions_per_Week: Acute/preventive: 4 post-op days, 2/day (Zhang 2025); chronic home use twice daily × 12 wk (Bremner 2021)
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Cervical neck placement (gammaCore); auricular variant at left cymba concha
Monitoring_Requirements: PCL-5 biweekly; heart rate during session; IL-6 stress response if available
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Throat discomfort; voice alteration; skin erythema; rare vasovagal response
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If no PCL-5 reduction after 6 weeks: review adherence and placement; consider auricular variant
Notes: Bremner 2021 PMID 33262253 primary (N=20 pilot, DOI 10.1016/j.jadr.2020.100079). Benzouak 2025 notes very low certainty — Grade C. Distinct from auricular taVNS (p-ptsd-002). `verify`: auricular-specific RCTs mostly pilot. Review_Status=Unreviewed.

Protocol_Name: HRV Resonance Frequency Biofeedback for PTSD
Evidence_Summary: Grade C (subgroup-only main effect). Pyne 2019 WAR study (N=342 National Guard) largest RCT; overall NS but HRVB arm lowered PCL in older soldiers (ES -0.97 to -1.03). Schuman 2022 pilot reduced Cluster B intrusion symptoms + depression. Burch 2020 (N=34 cancer survivors) reduced PTSD-spectrum symptoms.
Target_Region: Autonomic nervous system (cardiac) via resonance frequency breathing
Total_Course: 6–12 weekly clinical sessions + daily home practice
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: HRV biofeedback display + breathing pacer; chest-strap or fingertip PPG
Monitoring_Requirements: PCL-5 every 2 weeks; HRV LF/HF at baseline and post-course; daily HRV logs
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: No significant AEs; caution in severe cardiac arrhythmia
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Identify individual resonance frequency (5–7 breaths/min); escalate to weekly clinic sessions if home adherence <50%
Notes: Pyne 2019 PMID 30020511 (Mil Med, DOI 10.1093/milmed/usy171) main RCT — subgroup effect only, hence Grade C. `verify`: session counts vary across studies. Distinct from PRO-051 (GAD — Grade B). Adjunctive to trauma-focused CBT; not a sole therapy for severe PTSD.

Protocol_Name: taVNS / tcVNS for Chronic Pain (Auricular and Cervical)
Evidence_Summary: Grade B. Costa 2024 meta (k=15 RCTs) ES 0.41 (95% CI 0.17–0.66) favoring tVNS for chronic pain; auricular subgroup ES=0.42 (k=8). Heterogeneous device/parameters — stratify by pain phenotype before clinical rollout.
Target_Region: Left cymba conchae (auricular) OR cervical tcVNS
Intensity: NCT opaque on intensity (Costa 2024 pooled k=15 trials with heterogeneous mA; checked NCT04777500/NCT04503889/NCT06689345 arm descriptions, none expose mA — verify against published sub-protocol papers)
Session_Duration: NCT06689345 = 30 min; other underlying NCTs opaque on duration — verify per sub-protocol
Sessions_per_Week: NCT opaque on sessions/wk — verify per sub-protocol
Total_Course: NCT04777500 = 4 weeks total; other underlying NCTs opaque on total — verify per sub-protocol
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Auricular left cymba concha OR cervical gammaCore-style
Monitoring_Requirements: NRS/VAS pain weekly; BPI (Brief Pain Inventory) monthly; session HR monitoring
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Ear discomfort; skin erythema; cough reflex; rare vasovagal response
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If <20% NRS reduction after 4 weeks: review placement and escalate duration; consider alternative VNS target
Notes: Costa 2024 PMID 39131814 (Pain Reports DOI 10.1097/PR9.0000000000001171) meta-analysis anchor. Cross-checked 3 underlying NCTs via ClinicalTrials.gov v2 API: NCT04777500 (fibromyalgia, 4-wk taVNS), NCT04503889 (cymba conchae bilateral, ECO2-TENS device), NCT06689345 (30 min taVNS for chronic pain + post-COVID). NCT records opaque on mA intensity — verify flag retained: check published sub-protocol papers. Recommend stratifying by pain phenotype (neuropathic vs nociplastic) before rollout.
